items.title: 'You won't make it back': Trump's threat to Iran reps at peace talks

children.content: Donald Trump warns the Iranian delegation they might not return home from talks in Switzerland as the US and Iran argue over the fate of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Trump warns he may resume war as Vance holds talks with Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump has threatened to restart the US war with Iran at the same time his vice-president has been meeting with Iranian officials for the first talks under an interim peace deal.

image.altText: From left, JD Vance, Shehbaz Sharif and Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim al-Thani stand side by side in suits.

gallery.altText: Seyyed Abbas Araghchi walks, looking serious, surrounded by men in suits.

thumbnail.altText: Seyyed Abbas Araghchi walks, looking serious, surrounded by men in suits.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: 2.7 tonnes of cocaine found in elaborate bunker system at Sydney property

children.content: A record 2.7 tonnes of cocaine worth an estimated street value of $816 million has been seized at a semi-rural property in Greater Western Sydney.

image.altText: A series of plastic tubs filled with packaged drugs sitting inside a hole within a shipping container floor.

items.title: Victoria ended native forest logging. So where are these trees coming from?

children.content: Four Corners follows the logs, and the money, to uncover how efforts to end logging have shifted the timber trade across state borders.

image.altText: A photo shot through the windscreen of a car, showing a truck loaded with logs ahead on a semi-suburban street.

items.title: Tax system favours older Australians over younger, report finds

children.content: Australia's tax policy settings are leading to vastly different outcomes for people who have the same gross incomes but are simply different ages, a new report shows.

image.altText: A woman with an open laptop puts her head in her hands

items.title: Gus Lamont's grandmother denies involvement in disappearance

children.content: One of the grandmothers of missing four-year-old Gus Lamont says police have a theory that she buried her grandson after an accident — an allegation she describes as "ludicrous".

image.altText: A person wearing glasses with shoulder-length grey hair

images.altText: Mostafa Zico celebrates with his arms wide
New Zealand fans hold up a flag

articles.title: Vegemite levels help Socceroos' chef find out if players like his food

children.content: Chef Vini Capovilla has learnt a thing or two catering for the Socceroos at four World Cups. And one is finding out if they like his food.

image.altText: The Socceroos have a team barista in the hotel

articles.title: The most culturally diverse World Cup ever meets Trump's America

children.content: Fans from all over the world are being welcomed by locals across North America to celebrate the World Cup. But can it make a lasting difference in the US, where President Donald Trump has cracked down on immigration?

image.altText: A group of Haiti football fans cheer inside the stadium

children.content: Cabo Verde held Uruguay to a stunning 2-2 draw in Miami to give the World Cup minnows another memorable, magical moment, as New Zealand takes on Egypt. Follow live.

items.title: Egypt pulls ahead of New Zealand in crucial World Cup clash
Potential students allege visa scam tied to education consultancy

children.content: Aspiring students looking to study abroad in Australia say they've been left blindsided and with their futures in doubt after an education consultancy promised them migration and visa advice.

image.altText: A graphic pictures a thoughtful woman and text reading 'Visa expiring soon? Discuss your options'.

items.title: WiseTech Global shares slump more than 10pc

children.content: Australian stocks edge up while global markets remain on edge as US-Iran peace talks falter. Shares in WiseTech Global plunge more than 10 per cent in early trade. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Exterior shot of WiseTech signage.

items.title: Black swan 'wipe-out' predicted as dead birds reported along WA coast

children.content: More than a dozen dead birds that washed up on southern WA beaches are reported to authorities amid concerns an outbreak of avian influenza is spreading.

gallery.altText: A black swan on a river.

thumbnail.altText: A black swan on a river.

items.title: British PM expected to resign after challenger's thumping by-election win

children.content: Less than two years after being elected in a landslide, the Labour PM is expected to announce his resignation to make way for another leader.

image.altText: Keir Starmer looking down slightly with a neutral expression, walking towards the camera.

items.title: Australia signs 'biggest' export deal with Canada for $2.5b radar

children.content: Australia has signed its biggest-ever defence export deal, with Canada purchasing surveillance radar technology in a $2.5b agreement. Follow live.

image.altText: Richard Marles radar Canada

items.title: There's more to housing demand than population growth

children.content: The problem for both the Coalition and Labor is that things are only going to get more difficult for ordinary families in the years ahead, and the economic environment is likely to keep favouring One Nation's message.

items.title: Sydney Swan Taylor Adams announces AFL retirement

items.title: Green drought like 'trying to live on watermelon' for livestock

children.content: A splash of green has returned to New South Wales paddocks that were bare and barren just weeks ago, but the soil is still depleted and so are farmers' bank accounts.

items.title: Signs of life in Ningaloo Reef's baby coral after damaging year

children.content: After suffering its worst bleaching event on record, only to be battered by Cyclone Narelle six months later, the World Heritage-listed reef is showing its first signs of recovery thanks to the efforts of marine scientists.

image.altText: Marine researchers goes underwater to place spawning cones to gather and preserve coral reefs.

items.title: Think writing off something on tax makes it free? It doesn't

items.title: Auction clearance rates fall to lowest level in six years

items.title: This dog's cancer treatment 'excited' the US health boss but humans face hurdles

children.content: Rosie the dog had her tumours treated by an mRNA vaccine developed with the help of artificial intelligence. Her owner had decided to explore treatment options using AI chatbots.

items.title: Gambling reform consultation slammed as 'charade' by health experts

items.title: Is it possible to have a monoculture? This WA town doesn't think so

children.content: A Muslim leader from a regional WA town says he would like to have a conversation with Pauline Hanson about her ideology following her comments calling for monocultural Australia.

items.title: How gambling companies 'conduct psychological experiments' on their customers

children.content: Betting ads on TV have been front of mind in Canberra lately, but experts say we're overlooking the real firepower behind Australia's gambling industry: cutting-edge, hyper-personalised, online tech.

items.title: France on 'red alert' as temps hit 40C in Europe heatwave

items.title: Queensland watch houses overcrowded nearly a year after internal review

items.title: Macadamia orchard area expands up east coast, doubling in size

children.content: The macadamia production area in Australia has more than doubled in the past decade, but the price is expected to hold strong for consumers.
